subject: Re: M Crashes during species import
body:
Turns out this happened during the 3D denoising so 1 gpu wasn't enough to 
provide all the memory. 
So, I added one more by gpu passthru and it successfully passed.
However, I'm getting the new crash msg during the refinement. (with 
multiple pop-up so lastcrash.txt cannot capture the log properly)
Here's the screenshot of the crash msg.
[image: Screenshot 2020-07-31 12.58.57.png][image: Screenshot 2020-07-31 
14.43.22.png][image: 20200731.png]
Any thoughts, insights, and suggestions are greatly appreciated.

Thanks.
hee jong
On Thursday, July 30, 2020 at 7:18:33 PM UTC-4 Hee Jong Kim wrote:

> After finishing the dialog, during the process, I got the following crash 
> log.
>
> 7/25/2020 7:42:29 AM:
> TensorFlow.TFException: OOM when allocating tensor with 
> shape[4,96,64,64,64] and type float on 
> /job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:GPU:0 by allocator GPU_0_bfc
>      [[Node: batch_normalization_1/batchnorm/add_1 = Add[T=DT_FLOAT, 
> _device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:GPU:0"](batch_normalization_1/batchnorm/mul_1, 
> batch_normalization_1/batchnorm/sub)]]
> Hint: If you want to see a list of allocated tensors when OOM happens, add 
> report_tensor_allocations_upon_oom to RunOptions for current allocation 
> info.
>
>      [[Node: l2_loss/_445 = _Recv[client_terminated=false, 
> recv_device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:CPU:0", 
> send_device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:GPU:0", 
> send_device_incarnation=1, tensor_name="edge_9125_l2_loss", 
> tensor_type=DT_FLOAT, 
> _device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:CPU:0"]()]]
> Hint: If you want to see a list of allocated tensors when OOM happens, add

> info.

> Is it Out of Memory issue?
>
> Thanks,
> heejong
